frameworks.name: neuromodulation of cortical state
frameworks.one_line: LC-NE release reshapes coordinated cortical activity in a context-dependent way

frameworks.name: noise correlations / coordinated population activity
frameworks.one_line: pairwise spike-count correlations (rsc) as the readout of network state

frameworks.name: arousal / pupil-linked brain state
frameworks.one_line: pupil phase as an external index of LC activation and arousal

core_question: How do ongoing versus event-evoked locus coeruleus firing patterns relate to coordinated activity in anterior cingulate cortex of awake primates?
key_assumptions: LC spiking is a proxy for endogenous NE release to ACC
Pairwise spike-count correlations index coordinated network state independent of rate changes
Pupil diameter co-varies with LC activation and arousal
manipulations: Type of LC activity: spontaneous (baseline) vs. sound-evoked transient
Counting window size (100 ms to 1 s) for rsc
Behavioral context: passive fixation vs. fixation with randomly presented sounds
Pupil-phase conditioning on simultaneous LC/ACC recordings
key_findings: ACC pairwise correlations decrease when ongoing LC activity is high
ACC pairwise correlations increase following sound-evoked transient LC responses
These rsc changes are largely independent of ACC single-unit firing-rate changes
Both effects co-vary with pupil fluctuations that track LC activation
interpretations: Context-dependent LC-NE engagement modulates cortical information processing via coordinated activity rather than rate
Arousal-related fluctuations partly explain ongoing reconfiguration of cortical coordination
limitations_noted: Effects of artificial vs. endogenous LC activation could differ, and prior work directly linking the two is sparse
